title: To Kill a Mockingbird
author: Harper Lee
description: A gripping story of racial injustice in the American South, told through the eyes of a young girl.

author: George Orwell
description: A dystopian tale of surveillance, totalitarianism, and rebellion in a world controlled by Big Brother.

title: The Hobbit
author: J.R.R. Tolkien
description: Bilbo Baggins embarks on an epic adventure with dwarves and a wizard to reclaim a lost treasure.

title: Clean Code
author: Robert C. Martin
description: Essential principles for writing readable, maintainable, and efficient software code.

title: The Pragmatic Programmer
author: Andrew Hunt, David Thomas
description: Practical advice for software developers to improve skills and deliver better projects.

title: Designing Data-Intensive Applications
author: Martin Kleppmann
description: A deep dive into building reliable, scalable, and maintainable data systems.

title: A Brief History of Time
author: Stephen Hawking
description: Explores black holes, the Big Bang, and the mysteries of the universe in accessible terms.

title: Sapiens: A Brief History of Humankind
author: Yuval Noah Harari
description: Traces the story of humanity from the Stone Age to the modern era through key revolutions.

title: The Immortal Life of Henrietta Lacks
author: Rebecca Skloot
description: The true story of HeLa cells and their impact on medicine, raising ethical questions.

title: The Alchemist
author: Paulo Coelho
description: A shepherd follows his dreams across deserts in search of treasure and self-discovery.

title: Python Crash Course
author: Eric Matthes
description: Hands-on introduction to Python programming, data visualization, and web development.

title: The Elegant Universe
author: Brian Greene
description: Unlocks the secrets of string theory, relativity, and the quest for a unified theory of physics.
